compiled_state.title: As a debug-everything orchestrator, I can invoke fix-bug at orchestrator level without guard errors
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update fix-bug's sub-agent guard to recognize orchestrator-level invocation from the restructured debug-everything
**Why**: The nesting prohibition prevents fix-bug from dispatching its own investigation sub-agents when called as a sub-agent. Promoting fix-bug to orchestrator level requires the guard to distinguish between legitimate orchestrator-level calls (from debug-everything) and prohibited sub-agent-level calls (from Task tool).
**Scope**:
- IN: Modify fix-bug's SUB-AGENT-GUARD to detect actual Task tool nesting rather than caller identity; update guard test assertions
- OUT: Changing debug-everything's dispatch logic (Story 2); modifying fix-bug's investigation workflow

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, fix-bug invoked from debug-everything's orchestrator context executes without guard errors and can dispatch its own investigation sub-agents (BASIC/INTERMEDIATE/ADVANCED)
  ← Satisfies: "fix-bug runs at the orchestrator level (not as a sub-agent) with full Agent tool access"
- When this story is complete, fix-bug invoked via the Task tool (as a sub-agent) still triggers the guard and returns the error message
  ← Satisfies: "fix-bug runs at the orchestrator level (not as a sub-agent) with full Agent tool access"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Guard detection must use actual nesting context (Agent tool availability), not caller identity — future callers should not need guard updates

comments.body: Done definition update: test-sub-agent-guard.sh continues to pass — fix-bug does not gain a SUB-AGENT-GUARD (it uses Sub-Agent Context Detection). The existing delegation tests verify debug-everything uses /dso:fix-bug rather than deprecated fix-task templates.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All tasks complete: bdcd-a89b (RED test) + d71a-f206 (implementation). fix-bug context detection now uses Agent tool availability as primary method.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
